story: After winning a small lottery prize twice in a row, Elena is convinced she has a lucky streak and begins buying ten tickets every week. She believes her chances of winning again are higher than everyone else's because she has already won recently. She starts turning down overtime shifts to spend more time choosing ticket numbers.

beliefs: recent wins increase future win probability
she has special ability to pick winning numbers
her chances are higher than others
claims: winning twice means a streak is real
